decisions.detail: The authentication module was shipped last Friday with zero P0 bugs.

decisions.title: JWT token expiry set to 24 hours
decisions.detail: The JWT token expiry is set to 24 hours, as agreed in Sprint 2.

evidence.quote: Ben said the JWT token expiry is set to 24 hours as agreed in Sprint 2.

decisions.title: Password reset emails not rate-limited flagged

evidence.quote: Carol flagged that password reset emails are not being rate-limited.

decisions.title: Password reset rate limit of 3 per hour
decisions.detail: A maximum of 3 password reset attempts per hour was agreed upon in the security review on 2026-06-28.

evidence.quote: Max 3 reset attempts per hour was agreed in the security review on 2026-06-28.

decisions.title: API gateway choice and rate limit
decisions.detail: The API gateway will use AWS API Gateway with a 100 requests per minute limit per user, based on Ben's recommendation.

evidence.quote: The API gateway will use AWS API Gateway with a 100 req/min limit per user.
This was Ben's recommendation based on load tests.
API gateway: AWS API Gateway, 100 req/min per user cap

decisions.title: Mobile app team blocked by auth API spec
decisions.detail: The mobile app team is blocked until the authentication API specification is published.

evidence.quote: Alex noted the mobile app team is blocked until the auth API spec is published.

decisions.title: Auth API spec target publish date
decisions.detail: The target publish date for the authentication API specification is 2026-07-05.

decisions.title: Automated integration tests for all auth endpoints
decisions.detail: Carol wants all three auth endpoints (login, refresh, reset) to have automated integration tests before Sprint 3 sign-off.

evidence.quote: Carol wants all three auth endpoints (login, refresh, reset) to have automated integration tests before Sprint 3 sign-off.

decisions.title: Fallback behaviour if API gateway goes down
decisions.detail: An open question regarding the fallback behaviour if the API gateway goes down.

evidence.quote: What is the fallback behaviour if the API gateway goes down?

decisions.title: Refresh token invalidation on password reset
decisions.detail: An open question about whether refresh tokens are invalidated on password reset.

evidence.quote: Are refresh tokens invalidated on password reset?

implementation_scope: Implement password reset email rate limiting to a maximum of 3 attempts per hour.
Develop automated integration tests for login, refresh, and password reset authentication endpoints.

constraints.text: JWT token expiry must be set to 24 hours.
Password reset attempts are limited to 3 per hour for security compliance.
The AWS API Gateway imposes a rate limit of 100 requests per minute per user.
The authentication API specification has a target publish date of 2026-07-05.
The mobile app team is blocked until the authentication API specification is published.

architectural_decisions.text: The API gateway will use AWS API Gateway.

open_technical_questions: What is the fallback behavior if the AWS API Gateway goes down?
Should refresh tokens be invalidated on password reset?

acceptance_criteria: Given a user successfully authenticates, When a JWT access token is issued, Then the token's 'exp' claim is set to exactly 24 hours from the time of issuance.
Given a valid JWT access token, When a request is made using this token after 24 hours have passed since its issuance, Then the request is rejected with an 'Unauthorized' or 'Expired Token' error.

Given a user account, When a password reset request is initiated for that account and fewer than 3 requests have been made within the last hour, Then a password reset email is successfully dispatched to the user.

Given a user account, When a password reset request is initiated for that account and 3 or more requests have already been made within the last hour, Then the request is rejected, and no new password reset email is dispatched.
Given an authenticated user, When the user makes up to 100 API requests within a 60-second period, Then all requests are processed successfully (assuming valid inputs and endpoint availability).

Given an authenticated user, When the user makes more than 100 API requests within a 60-second period, Then all requests beyond the 100th are rejected with an HTTP 429 "Too Many Requests" status code.
test_scenarios: Verify a new JWT token is issued with a 24-hour expiry after successful login.
Verify access is granted using a valid JWT token within its 24-hour validity period.
Verify access is denied using an expired JWT token (after 24 hours and 1 second).
Initiate a password reset request for a user; verify the email is sent successfully.
Initiate 3 password reset requests for the same user within a 1-hour window; verify emails are sent for all 3 requests.
Initiate a 4th password reset request for the same user within the same 1-hour window; verify the request is rejected and no email is sent.
Initiate password reset requests to hit the 3-per-hour limit, wait over 1 hour, then initiate another request; verify it is successful.
Send 99 API requests from a single user within 60 seconds; verify all requests are processed successfully.
Send 100 API requests from a single user within 60 seconds; verify all requests are processed successfully.
Send 101 API requests from a single user within 60 seconds; verify the 101st request (and subsequent ones) receives an HTTP 429 response.
Verify successful password reset flow (e.g., requesting reset, receiving email, setting new password, logging in with new password).
edge_cases: Test JWT token expiry exactly at the 24-hour mark (e.g., 23h 59m 59s vs. 24h 0m 1s).
Test password reset rate limit behavior with concurrent requests from the same user.
Test password reset rate limit behavior from different IP addresses for the same user account.
Test API gateway rate limit behavior with extremely rapid bursts of requests (e.g., 101 requests in 1 second).
Test API gateway rate limit with multiple users making requests from the same shared public IP address (if IP is part of user identification for throttling).
Test the impact of server clock synchronization issues on JWT expiry and rate limiting.
unresolved_behaviors: The specific fallback behavior if the AWS API Gateway goes down needs to be defined (e.g., what error is returned, is there a degraded service, what is the user experience?).
It needs to be clarified whether refresh tokens are invalidated when a user performs a password reset, and what the expected behavior is for active sessions.
The detailed behavior of the refresh token flow (e.g., refresh token expiry, one-time use, rotation strategy, and error handling) for the 'refresh' endpoint needs to be specified.

decision_history: The JWT token expiry is set to 24 hours, as agreed in Sprint 2.
A maximum of 3 password reset attempts per hour was agreed upon in the security review on 2026-06-28.
The API gateway will use AWS API Gateway with a 100 requests per minute limit per user, based on Ben's recommendation derived from load tests.
All three authentication endpoints (login, refresh, reset) are required to have automated integration tests before Sprint 3 sign-off.
dependencies: The mobile app team is blocked until the authentication API specification is published (owned by Engineering/SA).
risks: Password reset emails are currently not being rate-limited (Risk of abuse, High Impact, Medium Likelihood given it's a known gap).
open_questions: What is the fallback behaviour if the API gateway goes down? (PM needs to ensure this critical system resilience question is answered and factored into planning.)
Are refresh tokens invalidated on password reset? (Requirements gap for PM/QA to clarify acceptance criteria.)
milestones: Authentication module shipped last Friday with zero P0 bugs.

system_requirements: The system SHALL ensure that JWT tokens expire after a maximum duration of 24 hours.
The system SHALL limit password reset attempts to a maximum of 3 requests per user per hour.
The system SHALL implement a rate-limiting mechanism for password reset emails to prevent abuse (inferred — needs confirmation).
The authentication module SHALL have automated integration tests for the login, refresh, and reset endpoints before Sprint 3 sign-off.
data_flows: User authentication requests SHALL flow from client applications (e.g., mobile app) to the Authentication API via the AWS API Gateway.
Authentication API SHALL issue JWT tokens to client applications upon successful login.
System SHALL send password reset emails to users, triggered by password reset requests.
interface_contracts: The Authentication API SHALL define endpoints for login, token refresh, and password reset.
The Authentication API specification SHALL be formally defined and published to unblock client teams (e.g., mobile app team).
The Authentication API login endpoint SHALL return a JWT token upon successful authentication, with a 24-hour expiry.

constraints.text: The AWS API Gateway SHALL enforce a global rate limit of 100 requests per minute per user for all API calls routed through it.
The Authentication API specification MUST be published by 2026-07-05.

architectural_decisions.text: The API Gateway for the system SHALL be AWS API Gateway.
JWT tokens issued by the authentication module SHALL have an expiry duration of 24 hours.

open_analysis_questions: What is the required fallback behavior if the AWS API Gateway becomes unavailable or goes down?
Are refresh tokens invalidated when a user performs a password reset, and what is the expected system behavior in this scenario?

stakeholder.business_summary: The core user login system, known as the authentication module, was successfully delivered last week. Key security measures have been put in place for password resets, but the mobile app development team is currently blocked awaiting final specifications for how to connect to the new authentication system.
key_impact: Core user login functionality is now available.
User account security has been significantly improved by limiting password reset attempts.
The mobile app's development timeline is currently impacted until the authentication system's technical details are finalized and shared.
System stability is safeguarded by setting limits on user requests, preventing overload.
major_unresolved_questions: What is our plan if the main entry point for our systems (API gateway) goes down? How will this affect user access?
For security, should a user's existing login sessions automatically end when they change their password?

issues.description: The QA View's 'unresolved_behaviors' section includes a point about the 'detailed behavior of the refresh token flow' needing to be specified. While a reasonable QA concern, this specific detail is not present as an open question, risk, or explicit requirement in the extracted decisions. Decision D008 only specifies the need for automated integration tests for the 'refresh' endpoint, not a lack of specification for its detailed behavior.
issues.suggestion: Remove this item from the 'unresolved_behaviors' list unless there is an explicit extracted decision or open question supporting it. Alternatively, rephrase it to reflect a QA 'suggestion' or 'future consideration' rather than an 'unresolved behavior' derived directly from the meeting notes.

review.reviewer_notes: The report generally maintains good traceability and quality across most role views. However, the QA view contains one critical hallucination, introducing an 'unresolved behavior' that is not directly supported by the extracted decisions. This needs to be corrected before publication.

evidence_quotes: Alex opened by confirming the auth module shipped last Friday with zero P0 bugs.
